result.markdown:
--- title: "Overview and workflow" subtitle: "The typed path from dataset to first-level fit, contrast, and group summary." jupyter: python3 execute: freeze: auto --- ## Core Workflow `fmrimod` combines timing information, event specifications, baseline terms, first-level fitting, contrasts, and group summaries under one typed workflow: ```text fmri_dataset -> fmri_lm -> contrast -> group_fit ``` A typical workflow starts with an event table, an acquisition grid, and a data object that keeps those pieces together. ::: {#575d5b93 .cell execution_count=1} ``` {.python .cell-code} import numpy as np import pandas as pd import fmrimod as fm from fmrimod.design_colmap import design_colmap events = pd.DataFrame({ "onset": [0, 12, 24, 36, 48, 60], "stim_type": ["face", "scene", "face", "scene", "face", "scene"], }) sf = fm.SamplingFrame(blocklens=[40, 40], TR=1.0) sf.blocklens ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=1} ``` array([40, 40]) ``` ::: ::: ::: {#ec4fb6a5 .cell execution_count=2} ``` {.python .cell-code} rng = np.random.default_rng(42) Y = rng.normal(size=(80, 6)) dataset = fm.fmri_dataset(Y, tr=1.0, events=events) dataset.n_timepoints, dataset.n_voxels ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=2} ``` (80, 6) ``` ::: ::: ## Event Model The formula creates one HRF-convolved regressor per stimulus level. The string form is a concise authoring surface; internally it compiles to the same typed design-spec tree used by the object builders. ::: {#d58fcdff .cell execution_count=3} ``` {.python .cell-code} model = fm.event_model( 'onset ~ hrf(stim_type, basis="spmg1")', data=events, sampling_frame=fm.SamplingFrame(blocklens=80, TR=1.0), ) model.design_matrix.shape ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=3} ``` (80, 2) ``` ::: ::: ::: {#b5501411 .cell execution_count=4} ``` {.python .cell-code} design_colmap(model)[["col", "name", "condition", "basis_name"]] ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=4} ```{=html}
col name condition basis_name
0 1 stim_type_stim_type.face stim_type.face SPMG1
1 2 stim_type_stim_type.scene stim_type.scene SPMG1
``` ::: ::: ## Baseline and Contrasts Baseline modeling and contrast testing are separate decisions. The design matrix stores task regressors; contrast vectors state the scientific comparison in that column space. ::: {#6ba5da2f .cell execution_count=5} ``` {.python .cell-code} columns = model.column_names event_face_vs_scene = np.zeros(len(columns)) event_face_vs_scene[columns.index("stim_type_stim_type.face")] = 1 event_face_vs_scene[columns.index("stim_type_stim_type.scene")] = -1 pd.DataFrame({"column": columns, "face_vs_scene": event_face_vs_scene}) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=5} ```{=html}
column face_vs_scene
0 stim_type_stim_type.face 1.0
1 stim_type_stim_type.scene -1.0
``` ::: ::: ## First-Level Fit Fit through the public first-level entry point. This keeps the dataset, compiled design, baseline, solver configuration, and contrast machinery in one inspectable result object. ::: {#95fe59f4 .cell execution_count=6} ``` {.python .cell-code} fit = fm.fmri_lm("hrf(stim_type)", dataset) fit.betas.shape, fit.tstat().shape ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=6} ``` ((3, 6), (3, 6)) ``` ::: ::: ::: {#2798c3d0 .cell execution_count=7} ``` {.python .cell-code} fit_columns = list(fit.model.design_matrix().columns) face_vs_scene = np.zeros(len(fit_columns)) face_vs_scene[fit_columns.index("stim_type_stim_type.face")] = 1 face_vs_scene[fit_columns.index("stim_type_stim_type.scene")] = -1 contrast = fit.contrast(face_vs_scene, name="face_vs_scene") contrast.estimate.shape, contrast.stat.shape ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=7} ``` ((6,), (6,)) ``` ::: ::: ## Advanced Topics The same design/data contract supports typed engine options, AR diagnostics, single-trial estimation, benchmark fixtures, and native group-level inference. Low-level matrix helpers remain useful for numerical canaries, but the flagship path should stay on the public seam. ## Next - [Linear model](linear-model.qmd) - [Contrasts](contrasts.qmd) - [Group analysis](group-analysis.qmd)
